mission.title: caddy: Strip path for reverse proxied requests
mission.description: Strip path for reverse proxied requests. This issue affects 13+ users.

steps.title: Identify caddy feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your caddy deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=caddy
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Strip path for reverse proxied requests

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant caddy resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
The [`reverse_proxy` directive](https://caddyserver.com/docs/caddyfile/directives/reverse_proxy) does not strip the path of the request before forwarding the request upstream. For example, in the foll

steps.title: Apply the fix for Strip path for reverse proxied requests
Confirm Strip path for reverse proxied requests is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Implementation of https://github.com/caddyserver/caddy/issues/3266

~This is incomplete/non-functional, still has debug prints etc. The general idea is there but it's not working as expected.~

Edit: Figured it out! Didn't realize I should be using `h.NewRoute()` which does the trick.

Caddyfile:
```
:80

handle_path /api/v1 {
    respond "API v1"
}

file_server
```

JSON:
```
{

    "h. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A working caddy installation or development environment.
